# 5. KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 Part A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

> **[KTU University Exam – May 2024 Model Paper]**

**Q1.** *List the four pillars of Mechatronics and identify each one inside a **digital camera** autofocus system.*

**Model Answer (3 marks):**

- **Mechanical:** Lens barrel and guide rails (the moving optical group).
- **Electronic:** CMOS image sensor, AF-assist LED, and op-amp signal conditioner.
- **Control:** PID / fuzzy controller that drives the voice-coil motor to a sharp focus (minimizes contrast metric).
- **Computing:** Image-processing algorithm evaluating contrast or phase-detection pixel output.

*(1 mark per correct mapping; full 3 marks for naming each pillar explicitly.)*

---

> **[KTU University Exam – Dec 2023 Model Paper]**

**Q2.** *In a fully automatic washing machine, what type of controller architecture is used, and which sensors drive state transitions?*

**Model Answer (3 marks):**

- **Architecture:** Finite State Machine (FSM) implemented in the **embedded microcontroller** (1 mark).
- **Sensors:** Water-level pressure sensor, **NTC thermistor** for temperature, **tachogenerator/encoder** for drum RPM, and door-lid interlock switch (1 mark).
- **State transition example:** *FILL → WASH* occurs when water-level sensor reports "FULL" and *WASH → RINSE* is triggered by an internal timer (1 mark).

---

## 5.2 Part B — Full 14-Mark Questions (Internal Choice)

> **[KTU University Exam – Module 4 Past Paper Pattern]**

### Question A (14 Marks)

**Q.A.(a)** Explain the **Mechatronic system architecture** of a **Pick-and-Place robotic arm** with a neat block diagram. Identify the role of each sub-system. **(7 marks)**

**Model Solution:**

A pick-and-place arm integrates all four mechatronic pillars:

1. **Mechanical Subsystem (2 marks):** Links, joints (revolute/prismatic), and end-effector (gripper). Gear reduction is used to amplify motor torque.
2. **Actuator Subsystem (1 mark):** DC servo motors at each joint (sometimes stepper motors for low-cost versions), driven by **PWM H-bridge drivers**.
3. **Sensing Subsystem (1 mark):** Optical encoders at each joint for position feedback, plus current sensors to detect gripping force.
4. **Controller + Computing (2 marks):** A microcontroller/DSP running a **PID** or **computed-torque** controller. The trajectory planner computes joint angles from Cartesian setpoints using **inverse kinematics**.
5. **Block Diagram (1 mark):** Reference → PID → PWM Driver → Motor → Gear → Link → Encoder → Feedback. *(See Section 4.3.)*

---

**Q.A.(b)** Derive the **transfer function** of an armature-controlled **DC motor** driving the robot joint. Given: $R_a=1\,\Omega$, $L_a=0.1\,H$, $K_m=0.05\,Nm/A$, $K_b=0.05\,V\cdot s/rad$, $J=5\times 10^{-4}\,kg\cdot m^2$, $B=2\times 10^{-4}\,Nm\cdot s/rad$. Find the **no-load steady-state speed** for $V_a=12\,V$. **(7 marks)**

**Model Solution:**

Step 1 — KVL on armature (1 mark):

$$V_a = R_a i_a + L_a \frac{di_a}{dt} + K_b \omega$$

Step 2 — Torque balance (1 mark):

$$J \frac{d\omega}{dt} + B\omega = K_m i_a$$

Step 3 — Eliminating $i_a$ in Laplace domain (2 marks):

$$\frac{\omega(s)}{V_a(s)} = \frac{K_m}{(L_a s + R_a)(J s + B) + K_m K_b}$$

Step 4 — At steady state, $s \to 0$ (1 mark):

$$\omega_{ss} = \frac{K_m \cdot V_a}{R_a B + K_m K_b}$$

Step 5 — Numerical substitution (2 marks):

$$\omega_{ss} = \frac{0.05 \times 12}{1 \times 2\times 10^{-4} + 0.05 \times 0.05} = \frac{0.6}{0.0002 + 0.0025} = \frac{0.6}{0.0027}$$

$$\boxed{\;\omega_{ss} \approx 222.22\,rad/s \approx 2122\,RPM\;}$$

---

### Question B (14 Marks) — *Alternative Choice*

**Q.B.(a)** With a neat block diagram, describe the **Mechatronic system of an Autonomous Guided Vehicle (AGV)** used in a smart warehouse. Discuss the role of **sensor fusion** in its navigation. **(7 marks)**

**Model Solution:**

1. **Mechanical (1.5 marks):** Differential-drive chassis with two DC motors + castor wheel; chassis must be rigid to support LiDAR mounting.
2. **Electronic (1.5 marks):** Motor drivers, LiDAR interface (UART/Ethernet), IMU (I²C/SPI), wheel-encoder interface.
3. **Control (1.5 marks):** Inner-loop **velocity PID** for each wheel; outer-loop **steering controller** for path tracking.
4. **Computing (1.5 marks):** Onboard computer (e.g., Raspberry Pi/Jetson) running SLAM, A* planner, and a **Kalman / Extended Kalman Filter** for sensor fusion.
5. **Sensor Fusion role (1 mark):** Fuses IMU (high-rate, drifts) with wheel odometry (good short term) and LiDAR (absolute but slow) to produce a robust pose estimate.

---

**Q.B.(b)** A **CNC milling machine** uses a **PID controller** to position its X-axis. Derive the **closed-loop transfer function** $\theta_{out}(s)/\theta_{ref}(s)$ assuming the plant is the DC motor from Q.A(b) and the controller is **$G_c(s) = K_p + K_d s$** (PD controller). State the advantages of adding a **derivative term** for CNC machining. **(7 marks)**

**Model Solution:**

Step 1 — Plant TF (1 mark, carried from Q.A.b):

$$G_p(s) = \frac{K_m}{s[(L_a s + R_a)(J s + B) + K_m K_b]}$$

Step 2 — PD controller (1 mark):

$$G_c(s) = K_p + K_d s$$

Step 3 — Forward path (1 mark):

$$G(s) = G_c(s)G_p(s) = \frac{K_m(K_p + K_d s)}{s[(L_a s + R_a)(J s + B) + K_m K_b]}$$

Step 4 — Unity feedback closed-loop (2 marks):

$$\frac{\theta_{out}(s)}{\theta_{ref}(s)} = \frac{K_m(K_p + K_d s)}{s[(L_a s + R_a)(J s + B) + K_m K_b] + K_m(K_p + K_d s)}$$

Step 5 — **Why Derivative helps in CNC (2 marks):**
- The **derivative term $K_d s$** acts as a **predictive damper**: it anticipates overshoot by reacting to the *rate of change* of error.
- For a CNC milling machine cutting hard metal, **sudden load disturbances** (chip-load variation) appear as velocity-like errors; the $K_d$ term counteracts them *before* positional error grows, yielding smoother surface finish.
- It increases the **damping ratio $\zeta$** of the closed-loop poles without sacrificing steady-state accuracy (which derivative alone does not affect).

---

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Pitfall Callout:**
> 1. **Do NOT skip the block diagram** — it carries 1-2 marks and shows holistic understanding (CO4).
> 2. **Do NOT confuse "plant" with "controller"** in the closed-loop formula: $T(s) = G/(1+GH)$, **not** $G/(1+G)$.
> 3. **Do NOT forget to substitute numerical values** in the steady-state calculation; many students leave the answer in symbolic form and lose 1-2 marks.
> 4. **Do NOT use the raw pipe `|` inside tables**; KTU e-content templates (and your answer sheet scanning) may render it incorrectly — write $\vert$ in LaTeX.
> 5. **Do NOT ignore units**; the examiner specifically looks for "rad/s" or "RPM" at the final answer.

---

## 5.3 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember (Rapid-Revision Checklist)

- [x] **Mechatronics = M + E + C + T**, all four co-designed — never treat a case study as just mechanical or just electronic.
- [x] **Universal block diagram:** *Reference → Comparator → Controller → Power Amp → Actuator → Plant → Sensor → Feedback*.
- [x] **DC motor plant TF:** $G_p(s) = K_m / \{s[(L_a s + R_a)(J s + B) + K_m K_b]\}$ — derive from KVL + Newton's law.
- [x] **PID:** $G_c(s) = K_p + K_i/s + K_d s$ → P gives accuracy, I eliminates steady-state error, D damps overshoot.
- [x] **Type-2 system** (PI in loop on Type-0 plant) gives **zero $e_{ss}$** for step **and** ramp inputs — critical for pick-and-place / CNC.
- [x] **Case study mapping (must memorize):**
  - *Washing Machine* → FSM, NTC thermistor, level sensor, tachogenerator, induction motor.
  - *Pick-and-Place Robot* → DC servo + encoder + PID + inverse kinematics in MCU.
  - *CNC Machine* → G-code interpreter → trajectory planner → multi-axis servo loop.
  - *AGV / Drone* → Sensor fusion (IMU + LiDAR + encoder) + EKF/SLAM.
  - *Digital Camera AF* → Voice-coil actuator + image-contrast/phase-detection feedback.
  - *HDD Read Head* → Voice-coil + air bearing + high-bandwidth servo (~kHz).
- [x] **Steady-state speed of DC motor:** $\omega_{ss} = K_m V_a / (R_a B + K_m K_b)$.
- [x] **Always mention all four pillars** in any case-study answer to satisfy the KTU CO descriptor for *integration*.
- [x] **For KTU ESE 14-mark questions:** plan for ≈5 min block diagram + 4 min derivation + 3 min numerical + 2 min review.
- [x] **Use LaTeX, label all axes, state all units, and conclude with a boxed answer.**
